name: KIMBERLY CLARK CORP
business: Kimberly-Clark is a global company focused on manufacturing and marketing essential products made from natural or synthetic fibers using advanced technologies in fibers, nonwovens and absorbency. Its portfolio includes brands like Huggies, Kleenex, Scott, Kotex, Cottonelle, Poise, Depend, etc., holding No.1 or No.2 share positions in approximately 70 countries. The company sells its products for household use directly to supermarkets, mass merchandisers, drugstores, warehouse clubs, variety and department stores and other retail outlets, as well as through other distributors and e-commerce; for professional use through distributors, directly to manufacturing, lodging, office building, food service, and high-volume public facilities.

exposures.sentence: We are principally engaged in the manufacturing and marketing of a wide range of products made from natural or synthetic fibers and materials using advanced technologies in fibers, nonwovens and absorbency.

A breach or other breakdown in our technology, including a cyberattack, privacy incident, data incident or other event involving us or any of our third-party service providers or vendors could adversely affect our financial condition and results of operations.
E-commerce potentially intensifies competition by simplifying distribution and lowering barriers to entry.

catalysts.event: Formation of joint venture with Suzano and sale of 51% interest in IFP Business
catalysts.sentence: At the time of closing, which is expected to take place in mid-2026 and will only take place following the satisfaction of consultation requirements and customary closing conditions, including obtaining required regulatory approvals, Buyer will acquire a 51% interest in the Joint Venture for a purchase price of approximately $1.7 billion, subject to certain closing adjustments set forth in the Purchase Agreement, and we will retain a 49% equity interest (the "IFP Transaction").
The 2024 Transformation Initiative is expected to be completed by the end of 2026.
